entities.raw_quote: Corniger (“horn-bearing”) is king of the south...

attributes.days: First to Fourth day of second purification

entities.raw_quote: In prima die secunde mundacionis dicet sacerdos missam...

summary: This chunk details the hierarchy and attributes of terrestrial spirits including Labadau, Asmodeus, and Corniger's attendants, along with their ritual requirements like sulfur suffumigation and specific circles. It transitions to Book Five, outlining five treatises concerning divine vision, angels, and absolution, followed by detailed prayers for consecrating blood as ink for the Seal of God.
key_passages: Princeps eorum est Labadau. Eius coadiutor est Asmodeus, qui dat thesaurum indestructibilem cuiuslibet monete.
Corniger (“horn-bearing”) is king of the south, and he has four attendants in the four parts of the world: Trocornifer in the East, Malafer in the West, Euiraber in the South, Mulcifer in the North.
Deus invisibilis, Deus inestimabilis, Deus ineffabilis, Deus incommutabilis...
In hac operacione sed de angelis terreis duo isti circuli necessarii; iste primus, qui est concavus, est in quo veniunt advocati...
Benedicat te Pater ✠, benedicat te Filius ✠, benedicat te Spiritus sanctus ✠.
